channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe lithium miners ask for more time to build processing plants
item.description: Lithium miners in Zimbabwe have asked the government for more time to build processing facilities, ahead of a January 2027 ban on concentrate exports, an industry executive said on Thursday. Zimbabwe, Africa’s top lithium producer, has been pressing miners operating in the country to process more of the battery metal locally as it seeks to extract more economic benefit from the mineral. (via Australasia Global Mining News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Australasia Global Mining News

item.title: Shocking Details Emerge: Mutare Nightclub Breastfeeding Mother Brutally Beaten to Death by Her ‘Ben 10’ Husband
item.description: A Life Extinguished: The Tragic Cost of Domestic Violence in Mutare Mutare – In a harrowing incident that has cast a long shadow over the Chikanga neighbourhood of Mutare, a 41-year-old woman, Samantha Chuma, has died following a brutal assault allegedly perpetrated by her husband, Tawanda Muradzikwa, aged 34. The tragedy, which unfolded on a [...] The post Shocking Details Emerge: Mutare Nightclub Breastfeeding Mother Brutally Beaten to Death by Her ‘Ben 10’ Husband first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Citizens Coalition for Change defectors: It is my turn now to feel the pain
item.description: ‘After restoring 12 CCC candidates on the ballot in Bulawayo, my heart sank as some of them voted in favour of a vulgarity that cannot be atoned’ The post Citizens Coalition for Change defectors: It is my turn now to feel the pain appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: ZVAYENDWA as Parliament Votes YES for CAB 3 and the 216 Individuals Who Grabbed Zimbabweans Right to Choose Their President @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Zimbabwe’s Parliament Passes Contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 Zimbabwe’s National Assembly has officially passed the Constitutional Amendment (No.3) Bill, a legislative development that alters the framework for presidential elections and other key governance structures. On Thursday, June 18, 2026, 216 lawmakers, predominantly from the ruling ZANU-PF party, voted in favour of the bill. This [...] The post ZVAYENDWA as Parliament Votes YES for CAB 3 and the 216 Individuals Who Grabbed Zimbabweans Right to Choose Their President first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabweans in Cape Town seek repatriation @bulawayo24
item.description: Hundreds of Zimbabwean nationals have gathered outside Zimbabwe’s consulate in Cape Town, pleading with authorities to assist them in returning home amid growing fears of xenophobic violence and mount... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Mutsvangwa slams Chiwenga over remarks @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: ZANU PF spokesperson Christopher Mutsvangwa has launched a sharp attack on Vice President Constantino Chiwenga, accusing him of using criticism of Zimbabwe’s wealthy elite as a political tool in his r... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Chivayo granted Eswatini citizenship, diplomatic passport @bulawayo24 @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo says he has been granted Eswatini citizenship and a diplomatic passport by King Mswati III following what he described as a private audience with the monarch at... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Bill to extend Mnangagwa’s rule clears National Assembly, heads to Senate

item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Kenya denies awarding $1.2-billion airport expansion contract to controversial businessman Chivayo @wicknellchivayo
item.description: TRT Afrika Kenya has denied that a company linked to controversial Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo has won a multi-billion-US-dollar contract to expand the country’s main airport, Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA), in the capital Nairobi. This follows earlier reports that Chivayo’s company, IMC Construction, was part of a consortium that had won a $2.9-billion (375 [...] The post Kenya denies awarding $1.2-billion airport expansion contract to controversial businessman Chivayo appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: King Mswati grants Wicknell Chivayo a diplomatic passport during Eswatini visit @wicknellchivayo
item.description: He says passport ‘meant to facilitate ease of travel and doing business in Eswatini’ The post King Mswati grants Wicknell Chivayo a diplomatic passport during Eswatini visit appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: The rise, fall and resilience of Siqokoqela Mphoko: A liberation son’s long fight for dignity @bulawayo24
item.description: To understand Siqokoqela Mphoko, one must begin where he begins — in the shadows of the liberation struggle, where childhood memories were shaped by whispered news of comrades who never returned. Born... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe lithium producers plead for more time to complete beneficiation plants
item.description: ZIMBABWE’S lithium producers have appealed to the government to extend the January 2027 deadline for a ban on lithium concentrate exports, saying that most companies are still completing processing plants required under the country’s beneficiation drive. Speaking at the ongoing Chamber of Mines of Zimbabwe Annual Conference, Lithium Association of Zimbabwe (LAZ) Chairman and Mutapa [...] The post Zimbabwe lithium producers plead for more time to complete beneficiation plants appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
